# M1.5 Face Engine — Designer Reference Brief

**Status**: Superseded reference material only. **NOT shipping assets.**
**Created**: 2026-05-17 (during M1 PR 2 brainstorm)
**Target milestone**: M1.5 (post M1 PR 3 ship)
**Origin**: AI-generated by Imagen 4 (canonical idle) + Gemini 2.5 Flash Image / Nano Banana (8 expression variants, edited from canonical)

> 2026-05-19 update: Ryan approved a newer **Minimal OLED Eyes** direction for M1.5 Face mode. Use `docs/design-references/m1-5-minimal-oled-eyes/` and `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-19-ulooi-minimal-oled-face-animation-design.md` as the current direction. This older yellow chibi set is preserved only as historical context.

---

## What this folder is

A starting-point visual reference for commissioning a real character designer to produce the **9 production face sprite assets** for the Looi iOS app's Embodied Mode (landscape).

These PNGs were generated by AI tools as a brainstorm artifact. They communicate **style direction + expression coverage**, but they have intrinsic AI-generation issues that make them unsuitable for ship:

- **Inconsistent details across frames** — eye shape drifts (e.g. `03-surprised` came out as hollow rings instead of solid bigger ovals; `05-alert` has an oversized mouth line)
- **Resolution + edge quality** — soft anti-aliased AI shapes don't match the crisp vector polish a chibi character at this aesthetic level needs
- **Small artifacts** — light leaks, occasional unintended highlights, mouth proportions varying frame-to-frame
- **No layer structure** — flat raster only, no separation between eyes / mouth / glow for runtime mixing

A human character designer will produce vector + layered + frame-consistent assets in 1-2 days from these references.

## What this folder is NOT

- Production sprite assets for `Packages/LooiKit/Sources/LooiKit/Resources/faces/`
- A finalized art style — the designer may push it further (more polish, character details, animation hints)
- Locked palette / proportions — references give direction, designer brings craft

## Files in this folder

| File | State | Notes for designer |
|---|---|---|
| `00-canonical-idle.png` | Idle (default) | The "base" face. All other expressions are variations of this. Yellow solid oval eyes + small white smile arc on black face, 16:9 widescreen. Soft warm yellow ambient glow. |
| `01-curious.png` | Curious | Eyes shifted upper-right (looking) + tiny floating yellow `?` glyph |
| `02-happy.png` | Happy | Eyes become closed upward crescents `^^` + slightly bigger smile + warmer halo |
| `03-surprised.png` | Surprised | Eyes wider/bigger + tiny `o` mouth. **AI got this wrong (hollow rings) — please use solid bigger ovals** |
| `04-sleepy.png` | Sleepy | Eyes closed downward `‿‿` + floating `Z z` glyph + cool blue ambient |
| `05-alert.png` | Alert | Narrowed eyes + small inward-slant brows + tight straight mouth + warm orange ambient. **AI made the mouth too thick — please make it short subtle line** |
| `06-blink.png` | Blink | Two thin short horizontal yellow lines (50ms transient frame) |
| `07-sad.png` | Sad | Eyes closed downward white crescents + light-blue teardrops below each + slight downturn mouth + cool blue ambient |
| `08-focused.png` | Focused | Smaller sharper eyes + tiny black center pupil dot + straight short neutral mouth |

## Style brief for the designer

**Vibe** (per the user's reference images during brainstorm):
- Chibi / kawaii / cute, BUT NOT toy-like
- Premium minimalist — closer to Apple-quality character work, not LINE Friends mascot
- Restrained: emotion read mostly from eye-shape + ambient color, very subtle mouth
- Black face background is the canvas; yellow is the brand "alive" color; white is for special accents (teardrops, smile)

**References the user shared in brainstorm** (search the brainstorm log for fuller context):
- Real Looi robot hardware (green body + black face screen with glowing eye shapes)
- A 5×5 grid of robot heads with simple-glyph blue/red expressions on rounded TV-screen faces
- An 8-frame chibi character set (sit/walk/play/sad/happy/etc.) in dark gray body + yellow eyes
- Some simple kawaii reference: 2 white circles + tiny accent + tiny smile on dark face

**Technical deliverable spec**:
- 9 PNG files, 16:9 widescreen, **2532 × 1170 (iPhone 17 native landscape) at @2x → 5064 × 2340 source**
- Transparent background OR pure black (so Mood thermal tint can be `.colorMultiply`-applied at runtime)
- Layered source file (Figma or Affinity or PSD) with separable eye / mouth / brow / overlay-glyph layers
- Consistent character: eye sizing, smile proportions, glow style, color hex codes documented
- Bonus: 2-3 intermediate frames per expression for smoother SwiftUI Animation crossfade (45 PNGs total)

**Color spec inferred from references** (designer may refine):
- Background: `#000000` to `#0a0a0e` (very deep cool black)
- Eyes (idle/curious/happy/blink/alert/focused): `#FFD05A` (warm sunny yellow)
- Eyes (sleepy/sad): `#9BC6FF` (cool light blue) OR yellow with `.colorMultiply(.blue)`
- Smile: `#FAFAFA` (off-white)
- Teardrop (sad): `#5DBBFF` (light blue)
- Ambient glow: derived from eye color with 30-40% opacity at 60-100px blur radius

## Integration plan (M1.5 PR)

1. Designer delivers 9 (or 45) PNG assets + layered source
2. Drop PNGs into `Packages/LooiKit/Sources/LooiKit/Resources/faces/v1/{idle,curious,...}.png`
3. Update `Package.swift` to include resources via `.process("Resources/faces/v1")`
4. Implement `SpriteFaceRenderer: FaceRenderer` that loads `Image("idle", bundle: .module)` per expression
5. SwiftUI `.transition(.opacity)` crossfade between expressions
6. `Mood.tint` applied via `.colorMultiply(mood.tint)` for runtime color theming (so 9 sprites cover all mood × expression combos without designer doing N×N variants)

## What M1 PR 2 ships instead (without face)

Per re-scope decision 2026-05-17: M1 PR 2 = GestureLibrary + Drive sheet + Dreams Wallpaper + GestureRingOverlay + DevTools dual-mode shell. The face area in EmbodiedMainView is a **placeholder**: pure black ZStack region with a single mood-color soft glow circle. Family-visible TestFlight builds will have basic interaction; the "face is alive" magic ships in M1.5.

---

## Why we didn't ship AI-generated faces

Tested workflow during M1 PR 2 brainstorm (2026-05-17):
- Imagen 4 (Google's flagship image gen) for canonical idle generation at 16:9 aspect ratio
- Gemini 2.5 Flash Image / Nano Banana for editing canonical → expression variants (preserves character)

Results were close to reference style but not shipping-quality because:
1. Each generation has small drift (rings vs ovals, mouth size, glow tint)
2. Even with NB editing canonical input, character consistency across 9 frames degrades
3. Premium chibi craft requires human design judgment AI can't match yet
4. Cost + iteration time per AI iteration (~10s/image × 4-8 candidates × 9 expressions × N revisions) exceeds hand-craft time for a designer at this style complexity
5. AI-gen outputs are raster + flat; we need layered + vector for runtime color mixing

The AI-generated references in this folder ARE useful as a **visual brief** — they show the designer "this rough style + these 8 emotion mappings + 16:9 widescreen layout" without us having to draw stick figures.
